skill.name: mobile-developer
skill.description: Guides React Native, Flutter, and native mobile app development with architecture, performance, testing, deployment, and platform integration advice.
skill.summary: Build mobile apps with cross-platform architecture, native integrations, offline sync, and app store delivery guidance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found these were false positives in Markdown guidance text. SKILL.md contains mobile development domain terms and a backticked documentation path, with no executable code, prompt injection, data exfiltration, or malicious instructions found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Path Flagged as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The static finding at SKILL.md:26 flagged backticked text as Ruby or shell execution. The line is an instruction to open a documentation file path, not executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is plain Markdown text with a backticked file path. There is no shell syntax, Ruby code, interpolation, or instruction to execute a command.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Mobile Terms Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The static findings at SKILL.md:3, SKILL.md:37, SKILL.md:55, SKILL.md:60, SKILL.md:67, SKILL.md:73, SKILL.md:80, SKILL.md:167, and SKILL.md:205 match mobile framework and architecture wording. They do not describe MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are descriptive mobile development text. Review found no cryptographic implementation, algorithm recommendation, or code sample using weak crypto.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Domain Vocabulary Flagged as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The static findings at SKILL.md:31, SKILL.md:70, and SKILL.md:179 are descriptive mobile development capabilities. They do not instruct the model to enumerate systems, scan networks, or collect host information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines discuss mobile app expertise, hardware API access, and Android libraries. No reconnaissance workflow, target discovery, network probing, or data collection behavior is present.

content.user_title: Build Mobile Apps with Cross-Platform Guidance
content.value_statement: Mobile teams need clear architecture and delivery decisions across React Native, Flutter, iOS, and Android. This skill provides practical guidance for implementation, testing, optimization, and app store release work.
actual_capabilities: Recommends React Native, Flutter, native iOS, and native Android approaches for app requirements.
Guides mobile architecture choices, including Clean Architecture, MVVM, MVI, repositories, and modular design.
Plans offline-first data sync, local storage, conflict handling, and background sync patterns.
Advises on mobile performance improvements for startup time, memory, battery use, networking, and animation.
Covers testing, CI/CD, app signing, beta release, app store delivery, and crash monitoring workflows.
Provides guidance for platform services such as push notifications, deep links, payments, maps, camera, and biometrics.
limitations: It provides guidance and implementation plans, but it does not include executable project files by itself.
It cannot validate app store acceptance without access to the actual app, metadata, and review feedback.
It may need project context before choosing between cross-platform and native implementation paths.
It references an optional implementation playbook that is not included in the scanned file structure.

use_cases.title: Plan a Cross-Platform App
use_cases.description: Choose an app architecture, state management strategy, offline model, and platform integration plan before implementation begins.

use_cases.title: Improve an Existing Mobile App
use_cases.description: Identify performance, reliability, accessibility, and testing improvements for a React Native, Flutter, iOS, or Android codebase.

use_cases.title: Prepare for Store Release
use_cases.description: Plan signing, screenshots, privacy disclosures, beta testing, monitoring, and release automation for app store submission.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Mobile Stack
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose between React Native, Flutter, native iOS, and native Android for this app. Ask for missing requirements first.

prompt_templates.title: Design App Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a mobile app architecture for my requirements. Include state management, modules, data flow, offline behavior, and testing strategy.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Performance
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this mobile performance problem and propose a prioritized optimization plan for startup time, memory, network use, and animations.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Release Automation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a CI/CD and release plan for a production mobile app. Include signing, beta distribution, store submission, monitoring, and rollback strategy.

output_examples.input: I need an offline-first app for field workers on iOS and Android.
output_examples.output: A recommended architecture with local storage, sync queues, conflict resolution, background updates, retry behavior, and platform-specific constraints.

output_examples.input: Our Flutter app drops frames during long list scrolling.
output_examples.output: A performance checklist covering list virtualization, image caching, rebuild reduction, profiling steps, and validation metrics.

output_examples.input: We are preparing our first App Store and Google Play release.
output_examples.output: A release readiness plan covering signing, privacy labels, screenshots, beta testing, crash reporting, monitoring, and review risks.

best_practices: Share target platforms, app complexity, team skills, and release goals before asking for architecture advice.
Ask for verification steps with each implementation plan, including tests, profiling, and release checks.
Include security, privacy, accessibility, and offline behavior early in mobile feature design.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for a framework choice without describing product requirements and team constraints.
Do not treat cross-platform code reuse as more important than platform quality and user experience.
Do not defer signing, monitoring, privacy disclosures, or app store requirements until the final release week.

faq.question: Does this skill write complete mobile apps?
faq.answer: It can guide implementation and produce plans or code-oriented advice, but the scanned skill file is guidance only.

faq.question: Which mobile frameworks does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers React Native, Flutter, native iOS, native Android, Expo, Ionic, .NET MAUI, and related migration topics.

faq.question: Can it help with offline sync?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers offline-first architecture, local databases, background sync, delta updates, and conflict resolution.

faq.question: Can it help with app store release work?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers signing, store metadata, screenshots, beta testing, privacy labels, and release automation.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed static findings were false positives, and no malicious behavior was found in SKILL.md.

faq.question: What context should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide target platforms, current stack, app goals, integrations, offline needs, performance issues, and release timeline.
